items.title: More than 1,000 foreigners screened at hentian Klang during 'Op Mega'
items.summary: KLANG: More than 1,000 foreigners were screened by the Immigration Department during "Op Mega" around the Hentian Klang area today, in an effort to combat the presence of illegal immigrants in the country. It is understood that the Immigration Department placed full focus on the screening process to identify any involvement of external parties or employers harboring illegal immigrants - including nationals from Nepal, Bangladesh, Myanmar, Pakistan, and Indonesia - at the inspected business premises. All those detained were taken to the Selangor Immigration Department office for further investigation, and full details will be revealed tomorrow during a press conference by Immigration Department Director-General Datuk Zakaria Shaaban in Putrajaya.

items.title: Iran newspaper lists Trump, European leaders as revenge targets
items.summary: TEHERAN: A conservative newspaper in Iran known for its provocative tone has published a list of people to be targeted as revenge for the killing of supreme leader Ali Khamenei, including the leaders of the US, Israel and European countries. US-Israeli strikes killed the late ayatollah on Feb 28, the first day of the Middle East war, and his son and successor Mojtaba Khamenei has vowed revenge. The infographic includes US President Donald Trump, Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and British Prime Minister Keir Starmer, among others.

items.title: Form Five student hospitalised after suspected poisoning in school
items.summary: District police chief Superintendent A.W. Charan said police received a report on the incident the same day. He also called on the public to respect the student's privacy and refrain from interfering with police investigations.

items.title: Two men arrested, 2,000 yaba pills worth RM20,000 seized
items.summary: KOTA BHARU: Police arrested two men and seized 2,000 pills suspected to be yaba, worth approximately RM20,000, in a raid at the parking area of Medan Selera Bukit Yong, Pasir Puteh, yesterday. Pasir Puteh district police chief Supt Saari Yaacob said both suspects were arrested at 6.40pm by a team of officers and members of the Narcotics Crime Investigation Department of the Kelantan police contingent headquarters while they were in front of a Yamaha Y16ZR motorcycle without a registration number. He said the inspection found 10 plastic packages containing 2,000 pills suspected to be yaba weighing 221.41 grammes.

items.title: Former ruler Sheikh Hamad is remembered as Qatar's moderniser
items.summary: QATAR'S former emir, Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al-Thani, a sweeping moderniser who seized power in 1995 and broke with tradition to hand it over to his son 18 years later, has died at the age of 74. Qatar's top government body, the Amiri Diwan, announced that Sheikh Hamad had died on Sunday morning. The Qatar that the current Emir Sheikh Tamim bin Hamad Al-Thani inherited was already well on its way to being remade by his father.

items.title: RE sector set for H2 2026 rebound on solar, battery storage demand
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's renewable energy (RE) sector is expected to regain momentum in the second half of 2026 as project activity accelerates, supported by a stronger pipeline, rising data centre demand and growing adoption of battery energy storage systems (BESS), said Hong Leong Investment Bank Bhd (HLIB). The research house said major RE programmes, including the large-scale solar 6 (LSS6) and corporate renewable energy supply scheme (CRESS), are expected to require storage capabilities, prompting developers to bring forward battery procurement plans in the second half of 2026. The firm said the rapid expansion of Malaysia's data centre pipeline would continue to be a key structural driver of renewable energy demand.

items.title: Consumer spending seen slowing before year-end rebound: CIMB Securities
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Consumer spending is expected to moderate in the third quarter of 2026 as festive-driven purchases seen earlier in the year taper off, according to CIMB Securities Sdn Bhd. In a note, the firm expects sales momentum to recover in the fourth quarter, supported by year end and festive spending, while government cash assistance and tourism continue to underpin demand for essential goods. CIMB Securities said government support would continue to cushion consumer spending in the second half of 2026, particularly for essential goods and products eligible under the Sumbangan Asas Rahmah (Sara) programme.

items.title: Aeon Bank expands Shariah-compliant financing for businesses
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Aeon Bank (M) Bhd has expanded its business banking offerings with the launch of Term Financing-i and Working Capital Financing-i under its Aeon Bank Biz platform, as it seeks to strengthen access to Syariah-compliant financing for Malaysian businesses. The two financing facilities are structured based on the Murabahah via Tawarruq concept, providing businesses with financing solutions for expansion and working capital requirements. The facility offers financing without collateral, repayment tenures of up to 60 months and direct disbursement into customers' Aeon Bank Biz Business Current Account-i.

items.title: Onn Hafiz sworn in for second term as Johor MB [WATCH]
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi was today sworn in for a second consecutive term as Johor menteri besar. The Machap assemblyman was reappointed after Barisan Nasional (BN) secured a commanding victory in the 16th Johor election to form the Johor government. The swearing-in ceremony was held at Istana Bukit Serene here before the Regent of Johor, Tunku Ismail Sultan Ibrahim.

items.title: South Korea asks North to help search for missing navy sailor
items.summary: SEOUL: South Korea asked the North today to help search for and return a missing navy sailor believed to have drifted across the de facto inter-Korean maritime border. The request follows reports that the sailor went missing this morning while a vessel was on patrol off South Korea's eastern coast near the Northern Limit Line (NLL). "As the South Korean navy is currently searching for the missing sailor, we request North Korea's cooperation, on humanitarian grounds, in the search and the individual's return," it added.

items.title: Lotus's Malaysia launches probe after pregnant woman is accused of shoplifting
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Lotus's Malaysia is conducting a thorough investigation into an incident in which a pregnant woman was accused of stealing at its supermarket in Selayang, Selangor, yesterday. Lotus's Malaysia, in a statement to Harian Metro, said it was aware of the incident, which went viral on social media today. "We are conducting a thorough investigation to obtain a full picture of what took place.

items.title: India's Tata Consultancy Services plans up to 8,900 AI deployment engineers, seeks AI acquisitions
items.summary: TCS is India's largest software services firm. Krithivasan's figures would translate to roughly 5,900 to 8,900 employees based on TCS's end-June headcount. AI: FRIEND OR FOE?

items.title: Onn Hafiz arrives at Istana Bukit Serene for Johor MB swearing‑in ceremony
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 12 — Machap assemblyman Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi arrived at Istana Bukit Serene here this afternoon, believed to be to attend the swearing-in ceremony as Johor Menteri Besar. The vehicle carrying the Johor Barisan Nasional (BN) chairman was seen entering the palace grounds at about 3.40pm, with members of the media gathered since noon to cover the ceremony. The swearing-in ceremony for the Johor Menteri Besar is expected to take place at the palace this afternoon.

items.title: DBKL allocates RM9.6mil to upgrade PPR Desa Tun Razak
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: More than 8,000 residents of Program Perumahan Rakyat (PPR) Desa Tun Razak will benefit from RM9.624 million worth of refurbishment works to improve their living environment. Kuala Lumpur Mayor Datuk Seri Fadlun Mak Ujud said in a statement today that the allocation would cover repainting works, fire safety and electrical system upgrades, as well as general maintenance involving 1,824 housing units. [[nid:1487069]] Fadlun said the upgrading project reflected DBKL's commitment to ensuring public housing remained safe, comfortable and well maintained.

items.title: Student arrested in alleged Cyberjaya prostitution raid
items.summary: CYBERJAYA: A university student's bid to earn extra cash during her semester break ended with her arrest after police raided an apartment unit allegedly used for prostitution activities here yesterday. The woman, believed to be in her 30s, claimed she was pursuing a double degree in Indonesia and had taken up the activity to earn extra money to support her studies. "I took up this gig to earn extra money to finance my studies.

items.title: Family sues ECRL-linked parties over alleged home safety risks
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A family has filed a RM1.33 million lawsuit against three parties involved in the East Coast Rail Link (ECRL) project, alleging that construction works have compromised the safety and structural integrity of their home. Hishamuddin Ahmad and his wife, Faizah Abdul Jalil, filed the writ of summons at the High Court here, naming project contractor China Communications Construction (ECRL) Sdn Bhd (CCC), project owner Malaysia Rail Link Sdn Bhd (MRL), and the Selayang Municipal Council (MPS) as defendants. Hishamuddin said the defendants were uncooperative when the family requested the Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) and Site Investigation reports in March 2024.

items.title: Rafizi vows Bersama will fight on as Johor polls was 'only the beginning'
items.summary: In a statement following the party's performance in the Johor election, he said that building a political movement to truly return power to the people would not happen overnight. "Yet, we keep moving forward, and you move forward with us," he said. "Every vote, every conversation, every rebuke, and every show of support is a trust we carry forward," he said, pledging that the party would learn from its weaknesses and take every lesson to heart.

items.title: Back for term two: Onn Hafiz arrives at Istana Bukit Serene for MB swearing-in
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Machap assemblyman Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi arrived at Istana Bukit Serene here this afternoon, where he is expected to be sworn in for a second term as Johor menteri besar. Onn Hafiz, who is also Johor Barisan Nasional (BN) chairman, arrived in the official menteri besar's vehicle at 3.40pm. Also present was BN chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi.

items.title: Woman killed in hit-and-run crash on KL-Seremban Highway
items.summary: KAJANG: Police are tracking down the driver of a white car who fled after crashing into a Perodua Bezza in a fatal accident on the Kuala Lumpur-Seremban Highway early today. District police chief Assistant Commissioner Naazron Abdul Yusof said the accident occurred at Km298.8 of the highway about 5.45am. "A white car travelling behind then collided with the Bezza before fleeing the scene," he said.

items.title: Global energy market deteriorating due to higher competition – Eni chief
items.summary: ROME: The situation on the global energy market could worsen in the short term amid declining global oil reserves and increased competition for energy resources, Claudio Descalzi, the chief executive officer of Italian oil and gas company Eni, believes. According to the top manager, European countries will need to purchase approximately 35 billion cubic metres of gas to bring their storage capacity to the target of 80 per cent. Descalzi also noted that global oil inventories are rapidly declining.

items.title: Ten of 34 women candidates secure seats in Johor state election sweep
items.summary: Of the total, seven candidates were from Barisan Nasional (BN), while three represented Pakatan Harapan (PH). Norlizah retained Johor Lama with a commanding majority of 16,344 votes in a three-cornered contest against PN candidate Aisah Esa and the election's youngest candidate, Danish Hossman Abd Rahman of PH. Fauziah also secured a landslide victory in Penawar with a majority of 15,776 votes in a three-cornered contest involving candidates from BN, PH and PN.

items.title: Anwar extends condolences to Qatari emir, people over leader's death
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has extended Malaysia's deepest condolences to the government and people of Qatar following the passing of its former leader, Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani who died today at the age of 74. "On behalf of Malaysia, I extend my deepest condolences to His Highness Sheikh Tamim bin Hamad Al Thani, the Qatari royal family, and the people of Qatar on the passing of His Highness the Father Amir, Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani," he said in a statement. Sheikh Hamad ruled Qatar from 1995 to 2013, when he abdicated in favour of his son, the current Emir Sheikh Tamim bin Hamad Al Thani.

items.title: Man arrested after GOF chase uncovers RM813,000 in suspected smuggled cigarettes
items.summary: General Operations Force (GOF) Southeast Brigade commander Senior Assistant Commissioner Ahmad Radzi Hussain said the seizure was made by a team from the brigade headquarters' Intelligence Branch. "Following a tip-off, the team spotted two suspected vehicles — a Proton Exora and a Nissan Grand Livina — at Kampung Pauh about 9pm. Radzi said checks on both vehicles uncovered 40 large packages containing 400,000 sticks of white cigarettes believed to be smuggled items.

items.title: Undocumented migrants scatter as Immigration raids Klang Bus Stand
items.summary: KLANG: Undocumented foreigners ran in every direction, with some even jumping out of buses, when the Immigration Department raided the Klang Bus Stand here this afternoon. The surprise operation, which began at about noon, triggered chaotic scenes as scores of foreigners attempted to flee or hide after spotting enforcement officers. The integrated operation was conducted by the Selangor Immigration Department together with the National Registration Department, the Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry, and the Putrajaya Immigration Department.

items.title: Johor MB swearing‑in: Media gather at Bukit Serene as palace keeps appointee’s name secret
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 12 — A Johor Menteri Besar is expected to be sworn in at Istana Bukit Serene here at 3pm today. According to an invitation circulated via WhatsApp, the Johor Palace announced that the swearing-in ceremony would take place at the palace this afternoon. The swearing-in of the Johor Menteri Besar is scheduled for 3pm today, July 12,” the invitation stated.

items.title: Sungai Perak turns bluish again, probe underway
items.summary: IPOH: The state government will await findings from the Mineral and Geoscience Department (JMG) and Department of Environment (DOE) before deciding on further action after Sungai Perak in Kampung Bukit Amarikan, Gerik, turned bluish again. Perak Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Saarani Mohamad said both agencies had been deployed to the site to conduct investigations and collect water samples, particularly to determine whether aquatic life in the area remained safe for consumption. "Yesterday (Saturday), JMG together with DOE went to the site to conduct studies and collect water samples following reports of fish deaths.

items.title: Teen escapes after Myvi plunges into Bachok river following suspected drowsy driving
items.summary: BACHOK, July 12 — A 16-year-old student escaped after the Perodua Myvi he was driving plunged into a river near the Pengkalan Petah water gate here yesterday, after he is believed to have fallen asleep while driving. Bachok Fire and Rescue Department operations commander Nor Azini Che Wil said the Form Four student from a school in Kota Bharu was travelling home after attending tuition classes when the incident occurred at about 3.10pm. Nor Azini said the vehicle had plunged into the river close to the bank, allowing the teenager to remain calm during the incident.

items.title: Son of Belgian wildfire victim disputes Spanish officials' account of warnings
items.summary: Belgian virologist Thomas-Wolf Verdonckt told Reuters on Saturday that he spoke to his father, 63-year-old businessman Stanislas Verdonckt, by phone just before 9 pm (1900 GMT) on Thursday evening as the fire advanced on the mountain village of Bedar in Spain's southeastern Almeria province. No information was provided," he said. Bedar Mayor Angel Collado said he urged the group including Stanislas Verdonckt to shelter in place.

items.title: Missing hunter found dead wrapped in canvas; two friends held
items.summary: SIPITANG: A man reported missing after going fishing and hunting in the Kampung Takula forest on July 3 was found dead, his body wrapped in canvas, following the arrest of two friends believed to be involved. Police believe the victim was accidentally shot while hunting. His body was found in a forested area about 1km from Kampung Takula after police questioned the two suspects on Friday.

items.title: Joss paper factory badly damaged in Rawang fire
items.summary: Selangor Fire and Rescue Department assistant operations director Ashrul Riezal Asbar said the department received an emergency call at 1.01pm reporting the blaze along Jalan Batu Arang, Sungai Bakau. He said firefighters from the Rawang fire station arrived six minutes later and found the fire engulfing a Class B factory measuring about 200 by 300 feet. He said the operation involved a Fire Rescue Tender, a Light Fire Rescue Tender, two water tankers and an emergency medical vehicle.

items.title: Johor polls disappointment for Pakatan, but Loke vows fresh push ahead of Negeri Sembilan election
items.summary: PH deputy chairman Anthony Loke said the coalition had anticipated a tough contest given the current momentum of support for the state government. Nevertheless, PH managed to secure eight out of the 56 state seats contested. “In the last Negeri Sembilan state election, PH won 17 seats, while BN secured 14.

items.title: South China Sea joint statement says China's maritime claims have no basis
items.summary: TOKYO: China's expansive maritime claims in the South China Sea have no legal basis, Japan, the Philippines, the United States and 11 other countries said in a joint statement on Sunday to mark the 10th anniversary of a landmark international tribunal ruling. The Philippines won the 2016 case at the Permanent Court of Arbitration that found China's sweeping claim of sovereignty in the South China Sea had no basis under international law, a decision that Beijing continuously rejects. ・Besides Japan, the Philippines and the United States, parties to the joint statement include Australia, Britain, Canada, Estonia, Germany, Italy, Latvia, Lithuania, New Zealand, Romania and Slovenia.

items.title: Pivotal US Republican Senator Lindsey Graham dies at 71
items.summary: Graham, 71, was a prominent Republican senator from South Carolina. Graham was elected to the US Senate in 2002. Graham recently served as chairman of the Senate Budget Committee.

items.title: Victory in Johor no guarantee of GE16 success, says BN's Saarani
items.summary: IPOH: Barisan Nasional's landslide victory in the Johor election should not be seen as a definitive sign that the coalition will triumph in the 16th General Election (GE16). Perak BN chief Datuk Seri Saarani Mohamad said past elections showed that voter sentiment could shift rapidly. He cited BN's victories in the Melaka election in 2021 and the Johor election in 2022, which were followed by a different outcome in the last general election.

items.title: Youth vs veteran in Johor polls: Felicia Poh, 28, wins Penggaram; Samsolbari, 65, holds Semarang
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 12 — At just 28, Felicia Poh Rui Ling became the youngest successful candidate in the 16th Johor state election, reflecting voters’ confidence in young leadership after winning the Penggaram state seat and retaining it for Pakatan Harapan (PH). The youngest DAP candidate in the election polled 24,522 votes to defeat Barisan Nasional (BN) candidate Boo Chin Leong, 65, who secured 20,385 votes, giving her a majority of 4,137 votes. The Ayer Hitam Umno division chief defeated Perikatan Nasional (PN) candidate Muhammad Syafiq Abdul Aziz and PH candidate Ramli Abd Hamid with a commanding majority of 14,679 votes.

items.title: Nga Kor Ming stands by pledge to resign over Najib issue as Puad challenges him to quit Cabinet
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 12 — Housing and Local Government Minister Nga Kor Ming today stood by his earlier pledge to resign from the Cabinet if former prime minister Datuk Seri Najib Razak were released without serving an appropriate sentence, as former Umno supreme council member Datuk Mohd Puad Zarkashi challenged him to make good on his words. In a statement on his Facebook, Nga also congratulated Barisan Nasional (BN) on its landslide victory in the Johor state election, saying the coalition’s two-thirds majority came with a heavy responsibility to govern with integrity. Shortly after Nga’s statement, former Umno supreme council member Datuk Mohd Puad Zarkashi called on the DAP vice-chairman to resign immediately instead of offering further explanations.

items.title: Policeman killed after Perodua Kelisa veers off Pasir Puteh‑Kota Bharu road, thrown from vehicle
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, July 12 — A policeman was killed after being thrown out of the car he was driving when the vehicle is believed to have lost control and veered off the road at Kilometre 30 of the Pasir Puteh-Kota Bharu Federal Road near Kampung Banir Belikong junction in Pasir Puteh last night. Pasir Puteh police chief Supt Saari Yaacob said in the 11.50pm incident, the 28-year-old victim, who was attached to the Setiu district police headquarters (IPD) in Terengganu, was believed to have been on his way home to Pasir Puteh from Kota Bharu. He said preliminary investigations found that the Perodua Kelisa driven by the victim lost control before veering off the road upon reaching the scene.

items.title: Selangor JPN vows strict action in Rawang bullying case, pledges full cooperation with police
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, July 12 — The Selangor State Education Department (JPN) is extending full cooperation in the investigation into an alleged bullying incident involving several secondary school students in Rawang, which gained public attention after going viral on social media. “The Selangor JPN takes serious note of the recent video circulating on social media regarding an incident involving student misconduct at a school in Rawang. The Selangor JPN will not compromise on any form of misconduct that could that could affect students’ safety and well-being,” the statement said.

items.title: Home Ministry to review EAIC report, vows stricter SOPs and vetting in citizenship approvals
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 12 — The Home Affairs Ministry (KDN) will review the findings and recommendations submitted by the Enforcement Agency Integrity Commission (EAIC) together with relevant agencies, including the National Registration Department (JPN), the Immigration Department (JIM) and the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM), to identify areas for improvement in the management of citizenship applications through naturalisation. In a statement today, KDN said the review would focus on strengthening standard operating procedures (SOP), documentation, coordination of work processes and governance mechanisms in line with public service best practices. “KDN wishes to emphasise that the power to consider citizenship applications is subject to the provisions of the Federal Constitution and is exercised with due caution, taking into account various factors, including public interest, national security and the facts of each case.

items.title: King meets Zahid and Onn Hafiz at Istana Pasir Pelangi following BN’s sweeping Johor victory
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 12 — The King, His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, granted an audience to Barisan Nasional (BN) chairman Datuk Seri Dr. Ahmad Zahid Hamidi at Istana Pasir Pelangi today. BN secured a landslide victory in yesterday’s state election, winning 48 of the 56 Johor state assembly seats contested. Meanwhile, Perikatan Nasional (PN), Parti Bersama Malaysia, Muda, Parti Orang Asli Malaysia (Asli), Parti Sosialis Malaysia (PSM), and independent candidates failed to secure any seats.

items.title: Johor’s tale of two margins: BN’s Permas towers at 29,505, Pakatan’s Simpang Jeram clings on by 170
items.summary: The biggest margin came from BN’s Permas candidate, Baharudin Mohamed Taib, who defeated PH’s Sharon Teo with 51,274 votes to her 21,769, gaining a majority of 29,505. That margin nearly quadrupled the 7,926 majority Baharudin posted in the 2022 election. Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi, BN’s Machap candidate, also delivered a commanding win by taking 20,382 votes over PH’s Nor Hafiz Roslan (5,007) for a 15,375-vote majority.

items.title: Terengganu says new tourism logo meets Syariah standards, turtle icon adapted for branding
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU, July 12 — The Terengganu state government insists its new Visit Truly Terengganu 2027 logo was carefully created to comply with Islamic principles, identity and design guidelines. State Tourism Committee chairman Datuk Razali Idris said that while Terengganu now boasts several new landmarks, the turtle remains the state’s enduring icon and has been kept as the logo’s centrepiece. Classic motorcycles on display at the closing ceremony of the Terengganu Classic Festival 2.0 2026.

items.title: Singapore President Tharman’s maiden Malaysia state visit begins July 13 with ceremony, talks, trade
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 12 — Singapore President Tharman Shanmugaratnam will undertake a three-day state visit to Malaysia from July 13 to 15 at the invitation of His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia. The Foreign Ministry said the visit reciprocates His Majesty’s state visit to Singapore on May 6 and 7, 2024. This will be Tharman’s first state visit to Malaysia since assuming office as the ninth President of Singapore on Sept 14, 2023.

items.title: Former education minister Maszlee wins Puteri Wangsa, emerges lone PKR success in Johor state election
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 12 — Former education minister Dr Maszlee Malik of Pakatan Harapan (PH) won the Puteri Wangsa state seat in the 16th Johor state election yesterday. According to the official results announced by the Election Commission (EC), Maszlee secured 41,821 votes, winning the seat with a majority of 5,744 votes. In the 2022 Johor state election, the Puteri Wangsa seat was won by Muda’s Amira Aisya Abd Aziz with a majority of 7,114 votes.

items.title: Syed Saddiq to learn fate Monday as Federal Court delivers verdict on prosecution appeal
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 12 — Muar Member of Parliament Syed Saddiq Syed Abdul Rahman will learn his fate tomorrow when the Federal Court delivers its decision on the prosecution’s final appeal against his acquittal on four charges involving criminal breach of trust, misappropriation of property and money laundering linked to funds belonging to Parti Pribumi Bersatu Malaysia’s youth wing, Armada. A three-member Federal Court bench comprising Court of Appeal President Datuk Seri Abu Bakar Jais and Justices Datuk Che Mohd Ruzima Ghazali and Datuk Collin Lawrence Sequerah is scheduled to deliver the judgment at 9 am. The Attorney General’s Chambers filed its appeal to the Federal Court the following day.

items.title: DAP to review Johor losses after losing Jementah, Tangkak and nine other seats
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 12 — DAP Johor will conduct a comprehensive review to determine why it lost several state assembly seats in the 16th Johor election, including Jementah and Tangkak. DAP lost 11 of the 17 seats it contested, including four previously held seats in Johor Jaya, Tangkak, Jementah and Perling. BN retained its grip on Johor, sweeping 48 of 56 state seats, while PH secured just eight.

items.title: Johor polls: 55 candidates lose deposits, including all Bersama hopefuls
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 12 — A total of 55 candidates lost their deposits after failing to obtain one-eighth of the total number of votes counted for the 16th Johor State Election yesterday. Meanwhile, seven candidates from the Pakatan Harapan (PH) coalition lost their deposits. In addition, all six Independent candidates, four candidates from the Malaysian United Democratic Alliance (MUDA), and the sole candidates representing Parti Orang Asli Malaysia (ASLI) and Parti Sosialis Malaysia (PSM) also lost their deposits.

items.title: EC to scrutinise voters who shared marked ballot photos after Johor polls
items.summary: ISKANDAR PUTERI, July 12 — The Election Commission (EC) will conduct a detailed review into incidents involving voters who uploaded photographs of marked ballot papers on social media during the 16th Johor state election, reported Sinar Harian. EC chairman Datuk Seri Ramlan Harun said the commission first needed to determine whether the images were taken by ordinary voters inside polling streams or by postal voters. Ramlan said the EC would examine the matter further to ensure such incidents, particularly involving ordinary voters, do not recur in future elections.

items.title: Wan Rosdy credits BN’s Johor victory to party machinery’s commitment and fighting spirit
items.summary: KUANTAN, July 12 — Barisan Nasional’s (BN) victory in the Johor state election was the result of the commitment and fighting spirit shown by the coalition’s entire machinery, said Pahang Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Wan Rosdy Wan Ismail. Wan Rosdy also thanked Johor voters for fulfilling their responsibility and giving BN the mandate to continue leading the state administration. “Congratulations BN.

items.title: Two suspected lovers arrested after police chase uncovers drugs in Perlis
items.summary: ARAU, July 12 — A man and woman, suspected to be lovers, were arrested and 419.82 grams (g) of drugs worth an estimated RM13,830 was seized in Taman Sri Wang here yesterday. The police then chased the motorcycle to Taman Sri Wang, where they were arrested,” he said in a statement last night. He said an inspection of the motorcycle revealed a black bag containing 13 transparent packets of various sizes containing what was believed to be380.45 g of heroin, as well as 13 packets containing 39.7 g of methamphetamine, along with a digital scale.

items.title: Female superbike rider dies after crashing into road divider after motorcycle event in Kuala Terengganu
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU, July 12 — A female participant of the Superbike Fighter 2.0 2026 programme was killed after her high-powered motorcycle crashed at Jalan Jambatan Kuala Ibai near here yesterday evening. Kuala Terengganu police chief ACP Azli Mohd Noor said police received a public report about the fatal accident involving an Aprilia superbike at about 6.45 pm. Investigation found the victim, travelling from Gong Badak in Kuala Nerus towards Dungun, lost control of her motorcycle while negotiating a bend and crashed into a road divider.

items.title: Man drives off with Honda Civic during test drive at Shah Alam car dealership
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, July 12 — Police are hunting a man who drove off with a Honda Civic car during a test drive at a used car dealership in Taman Sri Muda, Section 25, here last night. Shah Alam police chief ACP Sarudin Samah said the local suspect had earlier contacted the vehicle owner, expressing interest in buying the car, before arriving at the dealership at about 8.30 pm. “Initially, the victim refused to allow the suspect to test drive the car.

items.title: PAS respects Johor result, vows to strengthen fight ahead of GE16
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 12 — PAS respects the choice and mandate of the Johor electorate in determining the state’s leadership, says Johor PAS commissioner Datuk Mahfodz Mohamed. Meanwhile, Bersatu secretary-general Datuk Seri Mohamed Azmin Ali said the party would conduct a detailed review of the Johor election results to identify strategic measures to strengthen its political efforts. The 16th Johor State Election, held today, saw BN secure a simple majority after winning 29 of the 56 state seats as of 10.32 pm.

items.title: No seats, no deposits: Bersama suffers crushing Johor election debut
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 12 — Parti Bersama Malaysia (Bersama), making its electoral debut, suffered a major setback after all 15 of its candidates lost their election deposits in the 16th Johor state election today. The constituencies were Bukit Naning, Mahkota, Tiram, Permas, Larkin, Kempas, Kota Iskandar, Bukit Permai, Johor Jaya, Stulang, Perling, Skudai, Senai, Bukit Batu and Puteri Wangsa. In the 16th Johor state election, Barisan Nasional (BN) secured a fresh mandate to continue leading the Johor state government after winning 48 of the 56 state seats contested.

items.title: Perikatan wiped out in Johor as coalition loses all seats, including three incumbencies
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 12 — Perikatan Nasional (PN) suffered a crushing defeat in the 16th Johor State Election after failing to win any of the 33 state seats it contested. According to the official results published on the Election Commission (EC) website tonight, PN not only failed to expand its representation but also lost the three seats it won in the 2022 Johor state election, namely Bukit Kepong, Maharani and Endau. In Endau, PN candidate Hasnul Hakimi Hussein failed to wrest the seat from Alwiyah Talib, who represented BN in this election.

items.title: Anwar congratulates BN after Johor victory, pledges federal support for state
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 12 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today congratulated Barisan Nasional (BN) on retaining its mandate to form the state government following the 16th Johor state election. In a Facebook post, Anwar said the mandate entrusted to BN was a responsibility that must be carried out with integrity and in the best interests of the state and the people of Johor. Anwar, who is Pakatan Harapan (PH) chairman, also expressed his appreciation to voters and the people of Johor for fulfilling their democratic responsibility, and thanked election workers and party machinery for their dedication and efforts throughout the campaign period.

items.title: EC satisfied with smooth Johor election, says no untoward incidents reported
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 12 — The Election Commission (EC) has expressed satisfaction with the overall conduct of the 16th Johor state election, saying polling was carried out smoothly without any untoward incidents that could have affected the electoral process. EC chairman Datuk Seri Ramlan Harun said registered voters had been able to exercise their democratic rights and responsibilities during polling yesterday. Ramlan said voter turnout stood at 68.73 per cent, with 1,874,918 electors comprising early, postal and ordinary voters casting their ballots.

items.title: Pakatan admits Johor defeat after ‘shock’ eight-seat showing
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 11 — Pakatan Harapan (PH) has admitted defeat in the Johor state election after securing only eight of the 56 seats contested. At a press conference tonight, PH Johor state election director Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari acknowledged the coalition’s disappointing performance. Amirudin said PH had secured eight seats based on the EC results available as of 9.50pm, with DAP winning six seats and PKR and Amanah taking one each.

items.title: EC tally shows BN wins landslide Johor victory with 48 seats, Pakatan reduced to eight
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 11 — Barisan Nasional (BN) secured a landslide victory in the 16th Johor state election today, with the latest Election Commission (EC) tally showing the coalition winning 48 of the state’s 56 seats. EC chairman Datuk Seri Ramlan Harun, however, announced that BN had secured at least 29 seats, confirming that the coalition had crossed the simple-majority threshold needed to form the state government. PH suffered heavy losses after winning 12 seats in the 2022 state election, reducing its representation by four seats.

items.title: EC confirms unofficial results give BN simple majority in Johor
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 11 — Unofficial results announced by the Election Commission (EC) show that Barisan Nasional (BN) has secured a simple majority in the 16th Johor state election. During a Facebook livestream, the EC said BN had won at least 29 of the 56 seats in the Johor Legislative Assembly, giving the coalition enough seats to form the state government. The results remain subject to formal declarations by the respective returning officers following the completion of counting and tabulation.
